title: How Long Until Hackers Start Faking Leaked Documents?
summary: "It has long been easy to forge documents on the internet. It's easy to create new ones, and modify old ones. It's easy to change things like a document's creation date, or a photograph's location information. With a little more work, pdf files and images can be altered. These changes will be undetectable. In many ways, it's surprising that this kind of manipulation hasn’t been seen before."
body: There's nothing stopping attackers from manipulating the data they make public. In the past few years, the devastating effects of hackers breaking into an organization's network, stealing confidential data, and publishing everything have been made clear. It happened to the Democratic National Committee, to Sony , to the National Security Agency, to the cyber-arms weapons manufacturer Hacking Team , to the online adultery site Ashley Madison , and to the Panamanian tax-evasion law firm Mossack Fonseca . This style of attack is known as organizational doxing ....
